data.title: Samsung SDI narrows loss; shares jump on ESS demand
data.description:
South Korean battery maker Samsung SDI reported a smaller-than-expected Q1 loss, helping its shares extend a sharp rally. The stock jumped as much as 7% in early Seoul trading, driven by optimism around the company’s strategic pivot toward ESS for AI data centers and supply agreements with global automakers.

Samsung SDI reported Q1 2026 consolidated revenue of about 3.58T won (roughly $2.6B), up 12.6% Y/Y. Its operating loss narrowed sharply to 155.6B won, about 64% smaller than the same‑quarter loss a year earlier, coming in better than expectations.

The battery business saw resilient demand for grid‑scale energy storage systems (ESS)). Earnings were further aided by 80.5B won in U.S. advanced manufacturing tax credits tied to ESS production.

data.title: Komatsu reports FY results; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Komatsu press release (KMTUY): FY net income attributable decreased by 14.4% from the previous fiscal year to JPY 376.4 billion.
  • Revenue of JPY 4132.75B (+0.7% Y/Y).
  • The operating income ratio decreased by 2.3 percentage points from the previous fiscal year to 13.7%.

data.title: Greenland Energy prices $70M offering at $4 per share
data.description:
• Greenland Energy (GLND) priced a public offering of 17.5M shares at a price of $4.00 per share.
  • Each common share is being sold in combination with an accompanying common stock warrant. Each Warrant is immediately exercisable, will entitle the holder to purchase one common share at an exercise price of $5.00 per share, and will expire five years from the date of issuance.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to be $70M. The warrants have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Market and are expected to commence trading under the symbol "GLNDW" on April 28, 2026.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 29, 2026.

data.title: CBRE Group prices $750M senior notes
data.description:
• CBRE Group (CBRE) priced an offering of $750M aggregate principal amount of 5.250% senior notes due 2036.
  • The notes will have an interest rate of 5.250% per annum and are being issued at a price equal to 98.947% of their face value.
  • The company’s wholly owned subsidiary, CBRE Services will issue the notes, which will be guaranteed on a full and unconditional basis by the company.
  • The notes are expected to settle on May 4, 2026.
  • The company estimates that the net proceeds will be ~$737M and intends to use the net proceeds to repay borrowings under its commercial paper program.

data.title: Barclays GAAP EPS of 14.10p, revenue of £8.16B; reffirms all 2026 and 2028 targets, announces £500m buyback
data.description:
• Barclays press release (BCS): Q1 GAAP EPS of 14.10p.
  • Total income of £8.16B (+5.8% Y/Y).
  • Q126 Group RoTE of 13.5%.
  • Strong balance sheet with CET1 ratio of 14.1%.
  • The company announced intention to initiate a share buyback of up to £500m following the completion of the ongoing £1bn share buyback announced at FY25 Results.
  • Group financial targets: 2026 targets: Returns: Group RoTE of greater than 12%.
  • Capital returns: plan to return at least £10bn of capital to shareholders between 2024 and 2026, through dividends and share buybacks, with a continued preference for buybacks: Progressive increase in total capital returns versus 2025, Share buybacks announced quarterly, Dividends to be paid semi-annually, including planned £2bn dividend for 2026.
  • Income: Group total income of c.£31bn – Group NII excluding IB and Head Office greater than £13.5bn and Barclays UK NII of £8.1bn - £8.3bn.
  • Costs: Group cost: income ratio of high 50s in percentage terms.
  • Capital: CET1 ratio target range of 13-14%.
  • 2028 targets: Returns: Group RoTE of greater than 14% • Capital returns2 : plan to return greater than £15bn of capital to shareholders between 2026 and 2028, through dividends and share buybacks.
  • Income: greater than 5% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) 2025-2028.
  • Costs: Group cost: income ratio of low 50s in percentage terms. Cost target includes total gross efficiency savings of c.£2bn in 2026-2028.
  • Impairment: expect Group LLR of 50-60bps through the cycle.
  • Capital: CET1 ratio target range of 13-14% – IB RWAs of c.50% of Group RWAs.

data.title: Hut 8 prices $3.25B senior secured notes
data.description:
• Hut 8 (HUT) priced a $3.25B private offering of 6.192% senior secured notes due 2042 through its subsidiary Hut 8 DC.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 30, 2026.
  • Interest pays semi-annually starting November 15, 2026, with amortization from May 15, 2028.
  • This fully amortizing, non-recourse financing targets the River Bend data center project in Louisiana, featuring 245 MW of critical IT capacity plus a related substation. Proceeds will fund construction, reimburse Hut 8's (HUT) prior equity investments in the project, establish debt service reserves, and cover offering costs.

data.title: AB Electrolux GAAP EPS of SEK 0.55, revenue of SEK 2.79B
data.description:
• AB Electrolux (publ) press release (ELUXY): Q1 GAAP EPS of SEK 0.55.
  • Revenue of SEK 2.79B (-9.1% Y/Y).
  • Operating cash flow after investments amounted to SEK 69m.
  • EBITA amounted to SEK 280m, corresponding to a margin of 10.0%. Currency had an impact of –0.8 pp on the EBITA margin.

data.title: Nvidia expands India footprint with $130M lease deal in Bengaluru
data.description:
• U.S. chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) Indian subsidiary, NVIDIA Graphics, has leased about 760,000 sq ft of office space in Bengaluru’s Mahadevpura area.
  • The deal is a 10-year agreement valued at over $130M, underscoring its aggressive expansion in India’s AI and engineering ecosystem.
  • The company has also paid a security deposit of over $3.6M as part of the agreement. The lease covers 12 floors at Bagmane Capital’s Memphis South Tower and commenced on April 1, 2026.
  • The transaction ranks among the largest office leasing deals in Bengaluru and more than doubles Nvidia’s (NVDA) known office footprint in India.

data.title: Brixmor Property declares $0.3075 dividend
data.description:
• Brixmor Property (BRX) declares $0.3075/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.03%
  • Payable July 15; for shareholders of record July 2; ex-div July 2.
  • See BRX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Billerud AB Non-GAAP EPS of -SEK 0.69, revenue of SEK 9.82B; gives Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Billerud AB (publ) press release (BLRDY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -SEK 0.69.
  • Revenue of SEK 9.82B (-11.5% Y/Y).
  • Sequential sales volume growth of 9%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA SEK 525 million; Adjusted EBITDA margin 5%.
  • The board of directors proposes a dividend of SEK 2.00 (3.50) per share for the year 2025.
  • Outlook for Q2: Benefits from lower pulpwood prices and cost-saving program but challenging market conditions in Europe. Solid market conditions in North America. Broad-based price increases in both regions to mitigate cost inflation for chemicals and logistics. Sequentially higher annual maintenance shutdown costs.
